Notice Of Motion For Imposition Of Sanctions (Against Deft Coca-Cola)
Off Calendar Dec-05-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, Tentative Adopted. Plaintiff Lisa Munson-Cooter's motion for issue, evidentiary, and monetary sanctions is Denied in part, granted in part. The motion for issue or evidentiary sanctions is denied. The motion for monetary sanctions is granted. The Court finds that defendant failed to comply with the prior order. Defendant is ordered to fully comply with the order of September 21, 2006 within 10 days of notice of this order. Additional monetary sanctions in the amount of $2,840 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar Dec-05-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, Tentative Adopted. Plaintiff Lisa Munson-Cooter's motion to compel responses to request for production (sets #2, 3, 4), special interrogatories (sets #2, 3)
The motion is moot as answers have been served. The Court makes no rulings as to the sufficiency of any responses. The parties are ordered to meet and confer. The Court finds that defendant failed to respond in a timely fashion thereby requiring the filing of the instant motion. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $740 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Court Reporter: Mark I. Brickman, CSR #5527

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant China Basin Ballpark's motion to compel further responses to request for production is denied. As drafted, the request is overbroad, seeks information that is irrelevant to the subject matter of the action. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Motion For Order Staying Deposition & Quashing Deposition Notice Or For Alternative Protective Order & For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-20-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Robert J. Frassetto for defendant and moving party: China Basin Ballpark Company, LLC and San Francisco Baseball Associates L.P.
Philip C. Snell for plaintiff and opposing party: Lisa Munson-Cooter
COURT REPORTER: Dawn Stark CSR#7847

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for a protective order: Granted. The deposition of the slide attendant is stayed until such time as plaintiff has joined the other defendants. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to a mutually convenient time for the deposition.

All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. The Court declines to award sanctions despite the parties' unjustified delay in providing legitimate written discovery and unreasonable scheduling of the deposition.
Both parties to submit proposed Protective Order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES

COURT REPORTER: Dawn Stark CSR#7847

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories and request for production: Granted.
Defendant is ordered to serve verified responses and produce responsive documents within 15 days of notice of this order. Disclosure shall be pursuant to the terms of the proposed protective order set forth in Exhibit C of plaintiff's reply.
Both parties to submit proposed protective order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
